verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24delta1, TMP21) is a type I transmembrane protein of the p24/TMED family that cycles between the ER and Golgi as a COPI/COPII cargo receptor. Its primary steady-state localization is the ER-Golgi intermediate compartment (ERGIC) and Golgi membranes, with its extracellular/luminal domain facing the ER/Golgi lumen, not the extracellular space. Checking each contextual bucket: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented stress/activation-induced PM surface display; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no lineage-specific surface evidence; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — TMED10 is not a lysosomal protein; (4) dual_localization — no credible PM pool reported by surface biotinylation or flow cytometry on intact cells; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no shed ectodomain or surface-anchored partner documented. No therapeutic program targets TMED10 on the cell surface. It is an endomembrane-resident protein with no validated extracellular face exposure.
